title: Line Pressure Solenoid Circuit Check

figures_detected.description: Wiring schematic showing Solenoid connected to N-CVT C/U terminal 19
Illustration of solenoid connector disconnected and ohmmeter measuring resistance between terminal and ground
Illustration of vehicle with driver, brake pedal depressed and released, and DATA MONITOR display showing signal states including LINE PRESS SOL ON

nodes.text:
A
1. Turn ignition switch "OFF"
2. Disconnect connector from line pressure solenoid at end of transmission assembly
3. Check resistance between terminal #1 and ground.
Resistance: 10 to 20 Ω

N.G. → Open the oil pan and disconnect the internal connector
Check resistance between solenoid terminal and ground
Resistance: 10-20 Ω
Repair or replace wiring harness or connector between internal and external unit

B
Check output signal
1. Turn ignition switch "ON"
2. Start the engine
3. Select "DATA MONITOR"
4. Move selector to "D" range and accelerate to 10 km/h max and brake. When stopped, check that "LINE SOLENOID" changes from "ON" to "OFF"
Brake depressed: OFF
Brake released: ON
— OR —
Connect tester between N-CVT control unit terminal #19 and body ground move selector lever to "D" range.
Accelerate to 10 km/h max., stop and check that following values conform to standard:
Brake depressed: 0V
Brake released: Battery voltage
N.G. → Repair or replace harness or connector between N-CVT control unit and line pressure solenoid

items.label: Solenoid resistance (terminal #1 to ground, external connector)
Solenoid resistance (internal connector)
items.condition: Oil pan open, internal connector disconnected

items.label: N-CVT C/U terminal #19 voltage (brake depressed)
items.condition: Selector in D range, vehicle stopped after 10 km/h

content_blocks.text:
DATA MONITOR display states:
A.B.S. SIGNAL: OFF
TORQUE SIGNAL: ON
ENG TEMP SIG: OFF
IDLE-UP SOL: OFF
CLUTCH SIG: ON
LINE PRESS SOL: ON
RECORD

model_notes: Applies to N-CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission) models. Solenoid circuit check uses N-CVT C/U terminal #19.
